codeReviewIssues.description: The SAFETY comment for `unsafe impl Sync` says 'Sync is required because IrqMutex<State> (which is Sync) contains Vec<Box<Thread>>, and Box<T>: Send requires T: Send' — but this reasoning chain explains why Send is needed, not Sync. The actual reason Sync is needed is likely that IrqMutex<T>: Sync requires T: Sync (or T: Send + Sync). The safety claim itself is valid (Thread is never accessed concurrently, access serialized by IrqMutex), but the reasoning chain in the comment is slightly imprecise about which trait bound drives the requirement.

sharedStateObservations.description: Worker's skillFeedback suggests making the TDD exemption for documentation-only changes (SAFETY/AUDIT comments) more prominent in the kernel-auditor skill. The skill already mentions this ('TDD does not apply to documentation-only changes'), but the worker notes it could be more visible. Minor clarity improvement.

summary: Clean pass. The audit of thread.rs and thread_exit.rs is thorough and accurate. All 3 unsafe sites in thread.rs (Send, Sync, mem::zeroed) have detailed SAFETY comments with sound justifications. The deferred thread drop pattern (Fix 4) is correctly re-verified — scheduler.rs confirms exited threads go to deferred_drops in park_old (line 376) and are drained at the start of the next schedule_inner (line 337), when execution has moved to a different thread's stack. Drop ordering (guard page remapped before free_frames) is correct and well-documented. thread_exit.rs is pure safe Rust with comprehensive audit comments covering exit notification completeness, cleanup sequencing, and race analysis. 12 new tests (8 scheduler_state + 4 waitable) cover the exact patterns described in the audit: deferred drop unreachability, mark_exited idempotency, deschedule-exited no-op, full deferred drop lifecycle, kill+schedule race, wake-exited failure, reap from ready/blocked, and all thread_exit notification patterns. 413 tests pass, kernel builds. VAL-SCHED-002 and VAL-SCHED-005 are satisfied. One non-blocking observation: the Sync SAFETY comment's reasoning chain is slightly imprecise about which trait bound drives the requirement, though the safety claim itself is correct.
